features.summary: Deere reported strong Q3 results with 18.7% equipment operations margin and $5.32 EPS, driven by higher volumes and 8% price realization. Net sales increased 29% to $11.5B. The company acquired Bear Flag Robotics to accelerate autonomous capabilities and announced plans to purchase Deere-Hitachi joint venture excavator businesses. Early order programs for 2022 filled quickly, indicating robust demand continuing into next year with production rates for crop care products expected up strong double-digits.
features.management_outlook: Management expects strong demand to continue well into fiscal 2022, with order books extending through Q2 2022 for large tractors and full-year production slots filled for planters and sprayers. They anticipate supply chain challenges to persist through end of 2021 and into 2022, with $1.5B in additional material and freight costs. The company expects to be price-cost positive in 2022 and sees significant opportunities in autonomous solutions and precision agriculture technology adoption.
features.risks: Supply chain disruptions continue to impact production with more complexity expected through 2022, including material shortages, labor constraints, and logistics challenges across geographies. Raw material and freight costs increased $1.5B in 2021, with 45% hitting in Q4. The company faces potential production shutdowns if supply constraints worsen significantly. Currency volatility in international markets requires dynamic pricing adjustments to maintain margins.
features.key_drivers: Strong agricultural fundamentals supported by higher commodity prices and farmer profitability are driving equipment demand globally. Technology adoption rates reached all-time highs with ExactEmerge at 55% take rate and engaged acres growing to 290 million. The Bear Flag acquisition and excavator strategy position Deere for autonomous solutions and improved construction margins. Channel inventories remain at historic lows across all segments, supporting multi-year recovery potential.
